title: U.S. Education Initiatives Atlas
subtitle: A reusable initiative dataset and browser for education reforms, programs, and policy movements across the United States.

anchorDetails.evidenceBasis: School garden science trial included Washington sites
School garden science trial included New York sites
School garden science trial included Iowa sites

anchorDetails.initiative: Chicago test-based promotion and accountability reforms
anchorDetails.evidenceBasis: Chicago Public Schools reforms anchor to Illinois
School garden science trial included Arkansas sites
Texas retention study anchor for the broader policy family
Texas cluster randomized TX Sprouts study anchor
Statewide Mississippi literacy package and evaluation anchor

anchorDetails.initiative: School cellphone bans / phone-free school policies
anchorDetails.evidenceBasis: Florida district evidence anchor for school cellphone bans

initiatives.coreFocus: Career pathways, academy structure, labor market transitions
outcomes: Sustained earnings gains without reducing high school completion or college participation
initiatives.outcomeSummary: Sustained earnings gains without reducing high school completion or college participation

evidence.qualityNotes: MDRC long-run randomized evidence is unusually strong for a secondary career-pathway model and shows earnings gains without academic harm.

initiatives.coreFocus: Outdoor learning, nutrition education, hands-on instruction
outcomes: Some positive elementary reading and science outcomes with heterogeneity by grade and measure
initiatives.outcomeSummary: Some positive elementary reading and science outcomes with heterogeneity by grade and measure

evidence.qualityNotes: Randomized and quasi-experimental evidence suggests some academic and science benefits but effects are selective by grade and outcome rather than broad universal gains.

initiatives.coreFocus: Evidence-based reading instruction, structured literacy, early screening
outcomes: Adopted widely across states but not a single evaluable program
initiatives.outcomeSummary: Adopted widely across states but not a single evaluable program

evidence.qualityNotes: This is a research and policy movement rather than a single intervention so claims should stay descriptive unless tied to specific state implementations.

initiatives.coreFocus: Apprenticeship, paid work-based learning, career pathways
outcomes: Strong pathway design and descriptive positive outcomes with limited causal evidence so far
initiatives.outcomeSummary: Strong pathway design and descriptive positive outcomes with limited causal evidence so far

evidence.qualityNotes: Current open sources document program design and participant pathways better than causal impact so this should remain a promising but lightly evidenced apprenticeship model.

outcomes: Mixed but measurable achievement effects with subgroup differences
initiatives.outcomeSummary: Mixed but measurable achievement effects with subgroup differences

evidence.qualityNotes: Bleiberg finds positive initial math effects with meaningful heterogeneity and unequal subgroup benefits, so this should be treated as mixed rather than uniformly successful.

initiatives.coreFocus: School grades, accountability pressure, low-performing schools
outcomes: Positive math-focused effects with evidence of stigma and test-specific responses
initiatives.outcomeSummary: Positive math-focused effects with evidence of stigma and test-specific responses

evidence.qualityNotes: Florida A+ shows positive accountability effects especially in math but the mechanism appears tied more to stigma and pressure than to a clean policy channel and test-specific responses remain important.

initiatives.coreFocus: Grade-level reading, early intervention, promotion gates
outcomes: Mixed evidence with some reading gains and important implementation tradeoffs
initiatives.outcomeSummary: Mixed evidence with some reading gains and important implementation tradeoffs

evidence.qualityNotes: Policy family has supportive evidence from Texas and Mississippi-related work but outcomes vary by design and there are known tradeoffs around retention and implementation.

outcomes: Large grade 4 reading gains and national attention for literacy improvement
initiatives.outcomeSummary: Large grade 4 reading gains and national attention for literacy improvement

evidence.qualityNotes: State-level literacy package has a credible recent evaluation and a clear official implementation source but still bundles several mechanisms rather than isolating one lever.

initiatives.coreFocus: Technical pathways, employer partnerships, college credit
outcomes: Large internship and dual-enrollment gains with higher associate degree completion
initiatives.outcomeSummary: Large internship and dual-enrollment gains with higher associate degree completion

evidence.qualityNotes: MDRC lottery-based evidence is strong on internships dual enrollment and associate degree completion though implementation models vary across sites and states.

initiatives.initiative: No Child Left Behind annual testing/accountability
initiatives.coreFocus: Annual state testing, subgroup accountability, sanctions
outcomes: Clearer gains in math than reading with stronger effects for lower-performing groups
initiatives.outcomeSummary: Clearer gains in math than reading with stronger effects for lower-performing groups

evidence.qualityNotes: National quasi-experimental evidence shows stronger math gains than reading gains with larger benefits for lower-achieving and disadvantaged students.

initiatives.initiative: School cellphone bans / phone-free school policies
outcomes: Second-year test-score gains and lower absences alongside short-run suspension increases
initiatives.outcomeSummary: Second-year test-score gains and lower absences alongside short-run suspension increases

evidence.qualityNotes: Recent Florida evidence shows delayed academic gains and lower absences but also short-run discipline costs and the broader literature remains contested.

outcomes: Reduced debt and work burden with stronger persistence and STEM pathways
initiatives.outcomeSummary: Reduced debt and work burden with stronger persistence and STEM pathways

evidence.qualityNotes: Open impact report indicates stronger persistence completion and STEM outcomes but the program combines scholarship and support components and is not a randomized design.

initiatives.initiative: Chicago test-based promotion and accountability reforms
outcomes: High-stakes score gains plus strategic responses and uneven downstream benefits
initiatives.outcomeSummary: High-stakes score gains plus strategic responses and uneven downstream benefits

evidence.qualityNotes: The Chicago studies provide strong quasi-experimental evidence but also document test-specific gains strategic behavior and uneven benefits across grades and interventions.
